textByHash.0d80e5cfd752895fda399025010432c178a09da7: I'm Commander Lydia Fullore, leader of the Kourend Royal Guard.
textByHash.eb38d74c160d2d6ce1f95c2072bfc4ff2e1b234d: Ah, let me guess, you've been talking to Martin Holt?
textByHash.5664264563fb00f286080a740d2bc430776f7d28: Oh, I know him alright. Martin and I have quite the history.
textByHash.d5934368ab8baa9cdc286d8d19a524313766660c: He wouldn't. He has a very selective memory. I imagine he also didn't mention that he used to work for the Council that he hates so much.
textByHash.fdace7f57586dd7690d1a14fb322b94d31c438c5: Well I imagine his Royal Society friends wouldn't make so much of him if they knew where his loyalties used to lie.
textByHash.08a5f4178afd964c803b106e28073b545dc40627: Well I wouldn't be very good at my job if I did. Martin is always quick to point out the failings of the Council, but he conveniently forgets about all the good they've done.
textByHash.88b785d6830baf461af05308bb6d8b3dab69d7e4: You know that smell in Port Piscarilius?
textByHash.98ec78936e94724e20d4b5115a333538860defdf: Imagine that but ten times worse. That's what it was like before the Council had a new sewage system installed. The entire kingdom is now cleaner and safer because of them.
textByHash.9b03dac6f68b2a0b02e8ad0c4d708ac1072537d1: Not only that, but without the Council, you wouldn't even be stood here. It was under them that trade with your lands to the east was established.
textByHash.3f447c3581ab3fd38ca789fe01621823a574a345: Without those trade routes, you would have no easy way of getting here.
textByHash.e10f0a3c31792b084bb8eecb519be3d2997ba9c8: I could go on, but I have more urgent things to attend to.
textByHash.653fdc36d4ac859532c54563fb91de12c2e1b5fc: Well, since it's clearly no secret, yes. The former Councillor is a dangerous individual, so I want her back in a cell as soon as possible.
textByHash.1ddfa31048ebcfc50b3febc9c7587f8b492796a1: So, this is what Martin has put you up to! Well, no thank you. The last thing I need is one of his people getting in the way.
textByHash.91b04df57036ebad8f174ecb3ae69a275931605b: Hmm... you do have the experience. And I can't deny, the fact that you have the support of the protesters could be useful.
textByHash.6c68deb2235cb78ec5c23331ae79dd80be4df475: This whole situation with Councillor Hughes has caused enough civil unrest. We certainly don't need it to get any worse.
textByHash.4aa3e7cc18153fa065c00c109fcacf247fa0c71e: Alright, you can help. But for the duration of this investigation, you're working for me. What I say goes, is that clear?
textByHash.0c19fc9079e1b472771bc8d6ee88e6280793a8ff: Right now we don't have any leads on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'm heading over to her home to see if there's anything there that can help us. You should join me.
textByHash.6fefc4f927fb37c34564a1f6bf3807c87ea906fc: Tomas! What are you doing here?
textByHash.3fe83a635a90c6a9d5e6253bcfb62ebed8d227f8: The Kourend Royal Guard are conducting the investigation, Tomas. Your presence is not needed.
textByHash.3cd3e65a26896030096264dd4068b816f10db31c: That is not his call to make! I'm in charge of Council security. This investigation is mine to lead!
textByHash.02df447eba716aa6be76dce0e7c78875ecd6d416: Captain Ginea. I'm guessing Lord Shayzien sent you.
textByHash.64c3d98a43ca6d9a15ece4cd7362d8b860983baa: He's not alone there, but he doesn't need to worry. We have things under control.
textByHash.be2eb7306e88f0899a844732e0c771b6752baef2: You have my word. She'll be back in a cell before the day is out.
textByHash.a08146e7d1b3c29645bd55a967265019d2ade266: I can support the Council while not agreeing with every choice they make.
textByHash.87754cc52b9756af7d43ab7997702b8d97b9f42d: Would that be so wrong? I hardly know you.
textByHash.6dff13f8482594ad6bc2b9d3d16423f9bee81ea8: Here's some advice for you: focus on the investigation. Leave the politics to the experts.
textByHash.9cd27b8fd6794e911208cdd8c0c738feef5abb5c: Have a look around the house and see if you can find any hints on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'll ask around out here and see if anyone's seen anything suspicious.
textByHash.623947ca9273ef44fbf71543623c936489c43ece: Good luck. Don't let Lawry get in your way.
textByHash.0950a706b4300ee097d4eae662224a5c0c3a37c4: Okay, just let me know when you're ready to depart.
textByHash.1829fb30f8575b5c28643694f8c14744ea4133a2: Are you ready to head over to Councillor Hughes' home?
textByHash.ee998068d00118575d436a5b253f516c3151e9ec: Huh, not where I'd have expected. But maybe that's the point.
textByHash.dc4f13b8a6de55e56c171912e93788a675a73251: If you think that's where she is, I'd recommend you go and check it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.
textByHash.f144337b1d3793864ed59ed2ad30cf2a45e39c78: North of the River Molch, west of here.
textByHash.980615548b1dd012c836a89a115940bf909c0fb4: Seems a risky place for her to go. I'd have thought she'd pick somewhere quieter.
textByHash.924cb76a2b05af21dcc0ac7d6c8784145d586c29: If you're sure though, I'd recommend you go and scout it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.
textByHash.cfc139d2efafdcfbd2e59ee2069c6bd00eca7790: It's the pub in Lovakengj, north west of here.
textByHash.0bc52365bb6b9d75b4f699fd2f05e23ee0543aaf: You don't think Horace is helping her, do you?
textByHash.088dad96bd9c04d1c62be53a33daed334dc778a5: True. If that's where you think she is, I suggest you go and check it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.
textByHash.d8507fb83716971aec8e2fe4fef52faaac9c38c5: It's the general store in Hosidius, south east of here.
textByHash.fa428449a461d8427bccf51bd960340824743392: That's where most of her cultist friends spend their time, seems a bit obvious.
textByHash.1bce12b0423a2a55eacd1a52e986b2e6061c03b9: I guess it could still be worth a look. If you think she's there, you should go and scout it out. I'll keep an eye on things here.
textByHash.74f44ce50cadb9dc836a43dcbae9525c0712ae8f: On the outskirts of Shayzien, west of here.
textByHash.bcfc1844c7dcd1bfe9162ce651be305dd0611eb5: Well, keep looking around. There must be something here that can help us.
textByHash.0d0594339412ffd62e3be290682f3f856ab31907: It was two days ago, on the 26th of Fentuary.
textByHash.0b076a3660140b2ce9786ab90eed5cc74532e5a4: The start of the investigation was... delayed. I can't say any more.
textByHash.dacc38a87fffb591b821c0056986a86527a207ca: A demon? Well I guess the Disciples of Yama are known for that sort of thing. Still, that's going to be a nightmare to deal with. Can it get out?
textByHash.4b3a4ec2364a5165512e1adbbae6b95a47c5d07a: Good. Do your best to avoid it and we'll worry about it later.
textByHash.b744fbe65929797438a823fc270ffe7f0a50cf49: Have a look around the house and see if you can find any hints on where Councillor Hughes might have gone. I'll ask around out here and see if anyone's noticed anything suspicious.
textByHash.91a5622be765796e2512f0db6c8798f63e6e4067: Heard you were out here. Thought I'd come see how things are going.
textByHash.a35c110efd27fb69207d8e9bb2ddd577e1e1c460: Spying is a strong word. I just like to keep an eye on people I've not worked with before. Especially in a situation as delicate as this one.
textByHash.47f98398bab7646e21408770cb060929681b2938: And we'll be here when she does. Not bad.
textByHash.d5e66faf97c967133337b869824a6fe794f0da3a: It's over, Sophia. You should come quietly.
textByHash.bf544fbdfb7679414d6423b9392987cc8e110ec4: We're looking into how Sophia Hughes might have escaped. I'll let you know if we find anything.
textByHash.95dde47d1ceb407e835d7cf8a85264fd310074f2: I'm not sure what you're on about. Now, I'm sorry, but I need to get to work.
textByHash.74fcb7c66816a63084aeedbbd7e8b038b63d12df: Meet me just south of the castle.
textByHash.242882eec49ed4ddc8a9bcccf96a57c47dd9478e: Councillor Hughes was a deranged cultist.
textByHash.bf6e3de270a6d77b6d93971886cac643f8822e9f: Ever since King Kharedst IV died, there have been those who have questioned his death. People like Martin.
textByHash.879adb727e1528a1781aa3eb2abd4515e8ca5c2d: It was all just wild speculation, though. The King was old, and he died of an illness. It happens all the time.
textByHash.6bdccb212582567d4ddcfb9889954a5239f3e16b: Maybe. Or maybe it was someone else.
textByHash.28918b6bc7ce0bbb5e0d9ba8dcd16ea62865a56b: Rose. She was meant to take the throne after him, but she was too young. His advisors took over until she came of age.
textByHash.59cdc235f467742971609fdef6d2fe08b500dab8: Grief can do a lot to someone. Rose and her father were very close.
textByHash.33e634c0f280841f58e0b8aa077f7d80e886fe05: Listen to us! Are we really considering this? The Council rule Kourend. They have done so successfully for 20 years now.
textByHash.080efbc5930e461e143dafbf9f95093a833d541d: After what happened, public life was no place for her. The Council arranged for her to move away so she could escape all of that.
textByHash.877def1e3c77be0737ab6f3d97bac373ebb60f26: Look, I'm the commander of the Kourend Royal Guard. My duty is to the Council.
textByHash.4face4599b95fdc56e30915c554ce77196250c32: What kind of person would I be if I betrayed them?
textByHash.dd9e1aa184d619029ebaee4d9bc7d447a97c529d: Councillor Andrews may have been all nice to you, but he's furious with me. He's blamed me for what happened in Port Piscarilius. I have no hope of doing anything now without him knowing.
textByHash.6f275934f1fb23e2ba83cec06c75b8dbbf20c5a3: But you, on the other hand... You're just some adventurer as far as they're concerned. They won't be keeping an eye on you.
textByHash.c945d822a8afe2fb86cf5a6cf9f154ce73f2f80c: Rose. If she's still alive, then she's the key to the truth. You have to find her.
textByHash.51a3391f49e3ec3274e3dd6c98d7378534ea1989: I'm going to hate myself for this, but I know someone who could help.
textByHash.69093301a15c864511adc7529ee10ec9e5a94fbe: Martin. He's leader of Kourend's Royal Society. He knows more about the last royal family than anyone. If anyone can help you find Rose, it's him.
textByHash.9028ed695732fcb47b6cca96df45736908259cd1: Now, I need to get back inside, before someone finds out I'm missing.
textByHash.fcd7bbbe5ec2bf7516d8ddc31918e1f59a5fdc00: I really need to go. Speak to Martin, and good luck.
textByHash.da13394115af6ab854a5056ce4b044730bdad7c5: No time to talk I'm afraid. Didn't Councillor Andrews want you to speak to Martin Holt about his protest?
textByHash.40f0fc1febabdafdfb476c2c87e4770bcc763bf4: Is it... Is it really her?
textByHash.5ab21361e9a891da7515cabaf664b55ad8b11563: And you say she's not aware of... anything?
textByHash.6c0885cd1ed0c84a01029cbb896a8bf214089e23: What of the Councillor? Where is he?
textByHash.fa40b6ec80bfa5f4bdebe6669486eefcfdaf84bd: Liam... tell me it isn't true.
textByHash.02d43c28d5694ab798ed45eafe6dd4e01b294f39: I defended you! I defended all of you! And all this time, you've been lying!
textByHash.da04b61d2208c0ab440e4b95d0989484ffff5299: Look at her! Look at what you did to her! She was a child, Liam!
textByHash.2377bb7785860be5ee698c2228a139b33de42665: And you trusted him? Are you serious? Some random mage comes along and tells you to poison the King and you just... do it?
textByHash.30a3b3f9c1b571130b5654bc1d34b1308e0dbce7: Did it ever cross your mind that maybe this mage was the one that cursed him in the first place? How do you know he wasn't just using you?
textByHash.ecb739cc4474207245f78a6b655a0bb9df503a34: You could have gone to Lord Arceuus! You could have gone to Lord Shayzien! You could have gone to anyone apart from some random mage!
textByHash.19f071e1a08e805f95a68861e2d5b4fb434c7edc: You mean the condition you gave her?
textByHash.f02778e86704f2504b09ba64e1ddf29b3aa906c8: How do you know it's not the potions that are doing this to her?
textByHash.4408b263548630e38083663164ad74c5d2fd6c3e: This mage. We need to find out who he is.
textByHash.7c3633b1fa89b823e878a58fd6f816d4938e4fb5: Good luck out there. I'll keep an eye on this... I don't even have a word for him.
textByHash.821dbb576752d74b2d7107bd80ce841409f5342f: I'd rather not talk about it. We have work to focus on. There'll be plenty of time to contemplate later.
textByHash.c4ae00abe0bd6b36ad11cb1de8bce653673fa8f7: Terrible. I take it you heard about Martin?
textByHash.b32784f60f52c02ffb2d94172d474192e4927d7e: Such a stupid move. I told Councillor Andrews not to do it, but he listened to Lawry instead. Now the protestors are even angrier.
textByHash.c96c0162008dec41e777d23f25783da5fbffddcb: At this rate, it's only a matter of time before things get violent. I even had Councillor Unkar moved to the castle for his own safety.
textByHash.c2e9fb641957058195bd968647c195855ae41f99: Not that he deserves safety! What a mess this is.
textByHash.8b635850e6b2a3f5f9803e9e2cc9d936dfd5d15a: You need to find out who this mage is.
textByHash.8358bfe3a7c778762c1ad20cad9a89461abee5d8: Don't worry, I didn't kill him. I was sick of looking at him, so I moved him somewhere safe. He won't be going anywhere.
textByHash.4cfd5428bb2fbd870e695c724446447664aab5bd: The conversation? What was it about?
textByHash.a35beb49874d23c54ddecd2ce19557df6cf29ea1: Councillor Andrews was in on it from the start? That monster.
textByHash.a5755814d0659e437160688dcf268f44b1afeca7: The death? I heard about that.
textByHash.5c067ab5f798dbb040a5527b959d6885e19e6398: I'm not sure what any of this means, but it can't be good. We need to do something. But first...
textByHash.55a9cf5fbacd629acaf34c39f50a2b6d1d1f67e1: Rose. What do we do with her?
textByHash.44407f7d3b86a9d0fd4db9e9f34d039451855f8e: I did some research while you were gone. Liam wasn't lying. The poison they gave her irreversibly destroyed her mind. There is no cure.
textByHash.41501f3c8609d5046196efbcb61372d53ee18a9e: Someone else could make the potions, but all they do is prolong her suffering.
textByHash.ea2d24a96fa6d3e6236c0b20ae51a58c9bc60240: This is no life for anyone. The truth is, Rose died long ago. All that's left now is an empty shell.
textByHash.b9b9dfa48cf60c378bcb59b6d458709caf7a1090: Back when I was a girl, there was this Shayzien patrol that would go past our house each morning. Every day I would run out to wave at them as they went by.
textByHash.54df6b96a0746408cdc2c98b0368a7ba1dde825a: For as long as I can remember, I always wanted to join the army. 'Become a farmer or a chef,' my parents would say to me. 'The army isn't for you.'
textByHash.b44ed51c8a43437057f9f830cc121faac34eb0bd: But I knew what I wanted, and I knew nothing would stop me from getting it.
textByHash.064fc43591a8ab57bc4cb7cd9cb8964b2584dbd7: I saw some nasty things while serving the Council. I'd always tell myself that it was necessary.
textByHash.e0543e9fc29da99f949de9cbaf6a40cd23bc98f2: Whenever I thought about giving up, I'd ask myself who I'd be replaced with. Even if things weren't perfect, I was in the best place to improve them.
textByHash.d17a35426d112e399138d02f75d2df1aa0f7bea6: It was all a lie though, wasn't it? It's always been a lie.
textByHash.dcb8b9b1d1cb7b66b27c2c0001c8bbb9045246d8: I've spent most of my life serving a bunch of murderers; turning away from the truth because I was so scared of being wrong.
textByHash.22061076022fbc88f888c951cc6de73ef816a758: To think it was Martin who was right all along!
textByHash.fb38ae9843b519b939d1236320d5f2110a6470e5: We won't be able to do it alone. The Council still holds the power in Kourend.
textByHash.d426ee6ad26e92b289e41249c43659c922707608: We'd need to get them all on board.
textByHash.f07bbc70a002ec8ca336fee275717c0fd2d527f0: Alright, you gather the five leaders. Convince them to meet us at Xeric's Lookout, south east of Shayzien.
textByHash.8846eb63a52c786701ce4d9a74fa577ae226e0bc: It's where I left Councillor Orson. We won't be disturbed there.
textByHash.49f3e7915742c72587665c88016662081177fda9: It feels wrong, her being out here. She should have had a proper funeral.
textByHash.d6ceb91fac50f49c5edd3017a26aec1604f1f6b3: We'd better get going. Good luck.
textByHash.0dcf79dd0a08861014df369d7a9a116babe9883c: Have you spoken with all the leaders yet?
textByHash.388a0796645c6cdb0ebb192c9af45d480b2da7d6: Keep at it. We'll need all five of them on board. When you speak to them, tell them to meet us here.
textByHash.a93ed683746cf125c05738758b348e2f224776ba: Councillor Orson is down there. I'm keeping it locked, just in case.
textByHash.e0d4d74761dd167c1834f9a22a03e81567aa69dd: Well you did it. It's been a while since I've seen the leaders of all five houses together. They're downstairs waiting.
textByHash.5b158e8f2f05e9a6e0f1dd9a50439d8775265c64: He is. I told him about Rose.
textByHash.3618748c84e7a396c1568767bfd354d494130049: Indeed. Now, let's get down there.
textByHash.ab226488f247153488e51d626f8bbc161ccd53df: Indeed it is my lord, but I assure you, it's for a very good reason.
textByHash.aa088494041100c6f413974d5768062bcefcc6d4: Yes you can Liam, and you will.
textByHash.029cae385b8f4d88ce73fbf352b6b5a92411ffcc: This coward here killed her, just like he killed her father.
textByHash.57be7ea29e23bf15a4a0e62695d811168515d90c: I doubt it. The Council's plan worked because Rose was the King's only heir.
textByHash.c4d3751951d7512118485772dee1d3ff407c8539: The royalists do have a lot of support at the moment. But who would this monarch be?
textByHash.c71543c9582769214569b7afcb0f34b0f8ff4aba: Lady Lovakengj, you've been silent on all of this. What are your thoughts?
textByHash.800207437a36e4e9047669a66984d65712f4ffa0: This Royal Accord seems like our best bet. A clear document signed by all of them that determines the future of Kourend.
textByHash.28d83b19f2713ef87d2a31b3aa429a51b1213016: No, but there must be a way. They all seem to have some level of trust in you. Maybe you can convince them.
textByHash.4301c8d7ec2f37d422c55c3772532a34534d2883: This could quickly get interesting, but you need to do whatever it takes to get them back at that table. If even one of them walks away, we'll end up with a big problem on our hands.
textByHash.a220cce547136f29d7a5b4a79a550719417c17ee: He intrigues me. I find there's a certain level of arrogance when it comes to anyone from Arceuus. Living for a thousand years will do that, I guess.
textByHash.3788e57303fe87564548fa6d0d0854c37ab8529b: The thing is, he's not like that. He seems to have a lot more humility than the other leaders.
textByHash.39c08c2ab08fa2c72d21ffbe8c5b0c33fde1b411: Still, I find it hard to trust anyone who's been around that long. Think about what he must have seen and done over the years. He'll have more secrets than the rest of them combined.
textByHash.6628d236406eadd3770a86cc58c6b680d696684e: She's lazy and arrogant. She talks like she cares for the people, but one look at Port Piscarilius shows the truth. She only cares for herself.
textByHash.d0b53dc30eab25ed5601f963c28c7769b64ea2d9: Maybe she should start looking after her citizens then. Thoughts and feelings mean nothing when people are suffering.
textByHash.dc9bbd934c134a0c86923ecf1c96f7cf4ca1f68e: He's well-liked by his people, and he does genuinely seem to care for them. The world might be a better place if more leaders were like him.
textByHash.ce0efcecee6579f38a6d6326f708c1e1f0fa8ac0: That said, we should still be careful. Everyone has two sides, and he knew exactly what he was doing when he brought out the Royal Accord of Twill.
textByHash.0936fe76b4661255b0709a88a031861fc182c967: I have a lot of respect for him. He's achieved a lot in his short time as a ruler.
textByHash.b0144b186a87eb1dc0ea3469c3422def40ddd62a: Does there need to be a but?
textByHash.05c78891ee56a405f111c983e512f221e2be0db3: He's a man of war, not peace. You always need to be careful with someone like that. He's also the youngest of all the rulers. Age often brings experience.
textByHash.0c3dfeff1d22ce872801f69349f4c8da415b246a: I think she's the most dangerous of all of them.
textByHash.d6b5ba831dd0c78de9d7039c308351194b2195fe: She seems to be nothing more than a slightly senile dwarf with a love for sherry. If that's true, then she's not the kind of person who should be in charge of anything.
textByHash.8da18cb40a99e3bf20d74ac54e9caf089ec88080: But maybe that's not who she really is. Maybe it's all just an act. If that's the case, then she's managed to fool everyone around her.
textByHash.e60b06a524bff3ee99a260eacbfdd9e5a90b9c3f: Either way, she's one to keep an eye on.
textByHash.c6fc879f6ab4633cfe4b7bddb50b02b2f32a01d6: Keep at it. We need to find a solution that they all agree on.
textByHash.98540e0ac8808d863431306f8b27eb0d4952c2b0: That's not too suprising. The two of them are old friends. I've got nothing on him, but I can think of one person who might be able to help.
textByHash.b59ee971cc7c21c3852286353b3638089d0b6678: Exactly. His group has no doubt kept tabs on all of the councillors.
textByHash.57b7cbc95fb82d3d9dd7b81ffcff4c43e65d4cf9: No, but you can still talk to him. You've helped the Council enough that no one would think much of it if you paid him a visit.
textByHash.2331131b7bd666b720690a5583aa9f0f7be7c2e6: Nice idea. They took him to the Shayzien Prison, just west of the city.
textByHash.97c055a55b4b65f38978674cc51899d5e352a182: Have you spoken to Martin? He's in the Shayzien Prison, just west of the city.
textByHash.cdc7277af39530dd23cf945def39930af9192f68: Come with me. There's something you need to see.
textByHash.6e26fbc5fcebe7b559ac7b8ed01fd42c40e91d4d: What can we do? Even Lord Arceuus can't bring back the dead. He's gone, and that's that.
textByHash.0d47ca9e7e6682fb190fd1c6ceb105b2c5729379: Why did any of this have to happen?
textByHash.72107319b4e60cdd6a7847f630fb66bdc8ad5157: Does that mean you have good news?
textByHash.ea94713909271553e5556f0b7cc98d58e6ef273c: Well, we'll just have to make it work somehow. Maybe then we can finally end this nightmare.
textByHash.f78cf169ec55e6286f9cc2d5c8569dd10f10f5b2: I'll deal with this mess. Once I'm done, let's gather everyone downstairs again.
textByHash.4ebc87c019965028c5eac7c9541ac2a87544ddbe: Now that everyone is here, I think we can begin.
textByHash.6a346c84d6c0ea64a1ee100fa7897e7961109922: Not the attitude I'd take, but it's true that we can't dwell on this. We need to focus on stopping Councillor Andrews.
textByHash.78a0c7a7e0d11c90b4422c1143806dbf63f3d5f3: Lord Arceuus, let's start with you. What will it take for a new Royal Accord to gain your support?
textByHash.75a533d6cc723e947157cd6838d0f72e53df736f: You would not wish to rule yourself?
textByHash.d10eb79465a9216285ab049bc50fa88b371da1db: Lord Shayzien. What are your thoughts?
textByHash.5bf73d92defceb34f84980ec71d3e262ca987daf: Would you be open to taking on the role yourself?
textByHash.857f124c41b4e7df3c3bc108db3f7225d9ec5b0f: Are you sure? You seem to be a perfect candidate.
textByHash.909a0d92fd290ffb22b857a98a331f0bae2dc220: This decision will forever change the future of Kourend. If we go forward with this, the Hosidius family will be the new royal family of Kourend.
textByHash.54f35c274d60bf7c25fb4769bbfbf07e5c23064d: But Lord Shayzien? He seemed like a perfect candidate, but he didn't even want to be considered. What happened?
textByHash.84dff0489021ba034abfa43d2631ceab3cfa9647: Well, Lord Hosidius wants to have a word with you. Perhaps he can shed some light on all of this. He's downstairs.
textByHash.4ef81277cd0268c69b01b27768aaf526617165c2: Lord Hosidius wants to have a word with you. He's downstairs.
textByHash.1a05b137705d3fc22267cd6ed068b09f0d344b50: How did your chat with Lord Hosidius go?
textByHash.a2668299d50cd0de681afd71640b66b1ac38bd8b: We'll have to talk about that later. It's time to pay a visit to the Council.
textByHash.4f590e3ed11aa477ac24b8c59a629d062aa28c06: We'll join the five leaders and head to the castle. There, we'll tell the councillors everything we know and present Rose's Accord to them.
textByHash.a9b2a3356388a5987c80db18e0957988738da84d: They won't have a choice. Lord Shayzien commands the Shayzien Army, and I command the Royal Guard. They'll have no one to protect them.
textByHash.b3f10f93fe02377a953906ef9e1be9108954ae5c: They'll be imprisoned. It will be for the new ruler to decide their fates. As for that, we'll aim to hold the vote Lady Piscarilius requested as soon as possible.
textByHash.84398f0e09cfb6a6123e97366a3cd022791335a4: Assuming the people support Rose's Accord, we can have Lord Hosidius crowned within a few days.
textByHash.b656da0d8e4726733e4db6bff521214281d2201b: I think so. Many are already calling out for a king, and Lord Hosidius is popular across the whole kingdom.
textByHash.dc9267692e803135ab9581b00b3ab991a39d4af0: As soon as you're ready. From what I hear, the protest is getting close to breaking point. We can't waste any more time.
textByHash.d98ada9673d241d2d8b71a8f4b7b8264895c4bc0: Ready to pay the Council a visit?
textByHash.7cab384c56ae5c433a2510a91849c65e4b70405d: Soldiers of the Kourend Royal Guard, you serve the rightful ruler of Kourend! That is not this man. Stand down and step aside!
textByHash.3e83f3daa45c1d01db730928109979b9c5614ede: Don't take too long. Let me know when you're ready.
textByHash.d313aad0d1c4e2db4478dfeceb9d8a0f553b3b53: There you are! I was worried you'd miss all the fun.
textByHash.c92ffb14fe5b5d9efe871f61a6221b446b6506c5: You can say that again. You should have seen all the preperations needed. Food, drinks, entertainment, invitations... The list goes on.
textByHash.6b4a9b53e41efbcef048a9df93d655a59cf22a7f: You'd think security would be one of the hard parts, but I actually reckon our bit is pretty straightforward in comparison.
textByHash.f5261a79f245e414d12adc7609a4b6c60b1db26e: I don't think it's quite sunk in yet. It still feels like it was only this morning that Councillor Hughes escaped. So much has happened in such a short time.
textByHash.9ea63e2e83af28cf77db4a0d095557794ce8817a: Well, I for one am glad you didn't. We'd have never reached this point without your help.
textByHash.b7a5735e677f9804e671ce03b7a55c68edd89364: Oh, I'm going to need a lot to drink before I'm ready for that conversation.
textByHash.e6a72a42c49d63fd0aadb222c9beafbfc1b6e5fb: Anyway, have you seen our special guest?
textByHash.fea2a5c713e8737f28a9961b0818e66367b210f2: Zyanyi Arkan, the Sun Queen. She's the ruler of Varlamore, the kingdom in the south. This is her first visit to Kourend.
textByHash.d6054e6abd1d9274cbb3dbcb526f35659352c2b3: Indeed. The King personally invited her. He's keen to put an end to the animosity between the two kingdoms. Rumor has it she feels the same way.
textByHash.321c44f490cfbb14aceffd842820697531723700: They do. If the stories are true, they're the best fighters in Varlamore.
textByHash.e59cc3cb7e69460a396e88504ff3f2feec40e72b: Not of that armour, that's for sure. It looks very uncomfortable.
textByHash.9401582826edbcafa2ac8daea34093d173bf70a0: Ah, looks like they're done. You should go and see the King. He was pretty keen to speak to you.
textByHash.7d384f18e43495489845521a4fc38eabfefaf38b: It's been a rough few days since the coronation, as you can probably imagine. One moment we were crowning a new king, that next we were burying him. I'm doing okay, though. Work is a good distraction.
textByHash.dcb4250a425fa7abf7b99d2bd3f985e50c4c170c: Well Artur Hosidius is now the King of Great Kourend. I'm sorry you couldn't be there for the ceremony. After what happened, they felt something small would be best.
textByHash.08647861be5e5782e1960e0ef0174165f5522764: There's no shortage of potential suspects, but nothing solid. We've had the murder weapon sent over to Lovakengj to be examined. They have a theory, but want to do more work before they confirm it.
textByHash.98f02c94e350bf0b63791a6dc6d4c75e654d9fc8: I don't think so. Lord Arceuus is trying to find out more about Xamphur, but he doesn't have anything yet. For now, all we can do is wait.
textByHash.19991bdf46f8c520d772ddb1cb2b8d2428e4d255: And on that note, I wanted to give you a little something to thank you for all that you've done. Not just for Kourend, but for me as well.
textByHash.47f374eeeca97fdf2f7c1de45cb3290ec6206570: This is a bad situation we're in, but without you things would be a whole lot worse. Lord Arceuus also sent something over for you. He said you'd know what to do with it.
textByHash.b819b2972171c621b992957af188985b036fcbbd: Now, I'll let you get going. Thanks again, for everything.
textByHash.795f304ad5ecd0505601fd4cbadadaba7046d200: The Kourend Royal Guard protect and serve the ruler of Kourend. As their leader, I am responsible for ensuring that the Royal Guard are well trained and well supplied.
textByHash.7ac1e2611a9fc6a86a4a98aec5e4724caf071b76: No. Unlike the Shayzien Army, the Royal Guard serve the ruler of the kingdom directly.
textByHash.a3cc9910e1e86fb8b4b5898459172876243b7c82: The Royal Guard was established by King Byrne I. He felt that it was dangerous for Shayzien House to control the only military in the kingdom, so he decided to establish a small one of his own.
textByHash.2610bb42d191b7383b9d66d143b453c1a054521d: Ever since then, the Royal Guard have served Kourend's ruler.
textByHash.e424cecde6fdada9cb4bebe12830050708be32ca: The current ruler, or rulers as I should say, are the Kourend Council. They've ruled the kingdom for the past twenty years, ever since the last King died.
textByHash.e1bba6d93ccac17cd767834c6684c8aa838eaf11: Hey. I have something for you.
textByHash.34df104088a4f4a2ed273ea35dfb390e4255c8ac: It's still early days for King Artur. He was raised to rule, but he lacks the experience his father had. Still, he can't be any worse than the Council.
textByHash.419014aa7f398c654925ed08bf3d1ccff745b2e9: Nothing yet. For all we know, they could be one and the same. Lord Arceuus is doing everything he can to learn more about Xamphur, and the Lovakengj smiths are inspecting the murder weapon.
textByHash.009451f5c5b40f0d4ee33c9fa8f0674129f9bdf9: I'll let you know once we have something on either of them.
textByHash.afe245aca39a3d8774545a046f65d442972b8367: Good luck out there. Don't be a stranger.
